regions.us-ports: Today Miami reports ESE 5kt, clear skies and less than 10% rain probability, allowing routine embarkation, disembarkation and shore excursions with light ESE winds. Heat Advisory in effect for San Diego (temperature up to 90°F); Extreme Heat Warning in effect for New Orleans (heat index up to 115°F) and Galveston and Houston (heat index up to 116°F). Over the next 24 to 48 hours Miami is forecast SE 11kt, overcast, less than 10% rain probability on Day 1 then ENE 9kt, overcast, 10% rain probability on Day 2 for Port Everglades and Port Canaveral, and beyond 48 hours Day 3 shows ESE 11kt, thunderstorms and 22% rain probability to monitor for Tampa Bay.
regions.bahamas-central-caribbean: Today easterly winds E 4kt with clear skies and less than 10% rain probability support smooth embarkation, disembarkation and shore excursions in Nassau and normal pier operations. Over the next 24 to 48 hours Day 1 shows E 11kt and overcast with less than 10% rain probability and Day 2 shows E 11kt and overcast with 22% rain probability, factors to consider for Nassau, Freeport and Bimini tendering and dock scheduling. Beyond 48 hours Day 3 brings ESE 15kt with thunderstorms and 24% rain probability, indicating increasing convective activity that could require adjustments to outdoor shore excursions in Grand Cayman, Ocho Rios and La Romana.
regions.lesser-antilles: Today E 8kt east winds, partly cloudy skies and less than 10% rain probability support smooth embarkation and normal gangway operations at Barbados (Bridgetown) for scheduled shore excursions. Over the next 24 to 48 hours the forecast of E 12kt, overcast and less than 10% rain probability on Day 1 followed by ENE 13kt, drizzle and 13% rain probability on Day 2 indicates winds that could require tendering adjustments for St. Lucia and Grenada port calls. Beyond 48 hours E 13kt, drizzle and 19% rain probability on Day 3 signal persistent east winds that could extend harbor transits and prompt minor itinerary timing adjustments for Antigua and St. Maarten.
regions.los-angeles: Today WNW 3kt, partly cloudy, less than 10% rain probability, and calm conditions support routine embarkation and disembarkation at Los Angeles / San Pedro and Long Beach. Over the next 24-48 hours Day 1 W 12kt, overcast, less than 10% rain probability and Day 2 WSW 11kt, partly cloudy, less than 10% rain probability could increase vessel motion during tendering to Catalina Island and complicate gangway operations at Marina del Rey. Beyond 48 hours Day 3 SSE 10kt, clear skies, less than 10% rain probability will favor smoother open-water transits and improved visibility for on-deck activities across Los Angeles / San Pedro, Long Beach and Catalina Island.
regions.ensenada: Today WSW 2kt with clear skies and less than 10% rain probability supports routine embarkation and disembarkation at Ensenada and Punta Banda with minimal wind-related delays. Next 24 to 48 hours W 14kt, partly cloudy and less than 10% rain probability will affect Punta Banda and Islas Todos Santos, where stronger westerly winds may require adjusted tender schedules and caution during shore excursions. Beyond 48 hours Day 3's WSW 8kt, clear skies and less than 10% rain probability, following Day 2's WSW 8kt, foggy and less than 10% rain probability, indicates calmer WSW winds that should allow resumption of full excursion sequencing at Ensenada and Punta Banda.
regions.western-mediterranean: Today ESE 7kt, partly cloudy with less than 10% rain probability, supports on-schedule embarkation and disembarkation in Barcelona while open-air shore excursions and gangway operations proceed as planned. Over the next 24 to 48 hours ESE 8kt overcast with less than 10% rain probability (Day 1) then ESE 9kt partly cloudy with less than 10% rain probability (Day 2) allow routine pilot transfers and harbor maneuvering into Valencia, Palma de Mallorca and Ibiza. Beyond 48 hours Day 3 calls for ESE 8kt, overcast with less than 10% rain probability, keeping conditions steady for night arrivals, bunkering and fuel-transfer operations at Malaga, Cadiz, Lisbon and Gibraltar.
regions.central-mediterranean: Today NE 7kt, partly cloudy, less than 10% rain probability, permitting normal embarkation and tendering at Naples and Civitavecchia and supporting scheduled outdoor shore excursions. Over the next 24-48 hours Day 1 NE 8kt, partly cloudy, less than 10% rain probability; Day 2 SSW 9kt, partly cloudy, less than 10% rain probability will influence approach headings and berthing plans at Genoa, La Spezia, Livorno and Marseille. Beyond 48 hours Day 3 NE 11kt, overcast, less than 10% rain probability indicates a trend to slightly stronger NE winds and overcast skies that will prompt closer coordination of deck operations and reduce late-evening visibility for Corsica and Sardinia port calls.
